Apple___Apple_scab.description: Fungal disease causing dark, scabby lesions on leaves and fruits.
treatment: Apply fungicide sprays during early spring
Prune trees for better air circulation
Apple___Apple_scab.prevention: Apply preventive fungicide before bud break

Apple___Black_rot.description: Fungal infection causing fruit rot and leaf spot.
treatment: Remove mummified fruits and dead wood
Apple___Black_rot.prevention: Regular pruning and removal of infected material

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.description: Fungal disease with bright orange spots on leaves.
treatment: Remove nearby juniper/cedar trees if possible
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.prevention: Plant resistant varieties and apply preventive sprays

Apple___healthy.description: The plant appears healthy with no signs of disease.
Apple___healthy.prevention: Continue regular monitoring and good agricultural practices

Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.description: Fungal disease causing rectangular gray lesions on leaves.

Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.description: Fungal disease with circular brown pustules on both leaf surfaces.
treatment: Apply fungicides when rust is first detected

Tomato___Late_blight.description: Devastating disease causing water-soaked lesions and plant death.
Tomato___Late_blight.prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds and resistant varieties

Tomato___healthy.description: The plant appears healthy with no signs of disease.
Tomato___healthy.prevention: Continue regular monitoring and proper watering

Potato___Late_blight.description: The same pathogen that caused the Irish Famine. Rapid spreader.
treatment: Harvest early if infection is severe
Potato___Late_blight.prevention: Use certified seed potatoes and resistant varieties

Potato___healthy.description: The plant appears healthy with no signs of disease.

Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.display_name: Tomato - Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl Virus
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.description: A disease affecting Tomato plants. Diagnosis based on leaf patterns.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.prevention: Practice crop rotation and maintain garden sanitation.

Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.description: A disease affecting Tomato plants. Diagnosis based on leaf patterns.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.prevention: Practice crop rotation and maintain garden sanitation.

Brownspot.description: Fungal disease (Bipolaris oryzae) causing circular to oval brown spots with gray centers on leaves. Often associated with nutrient-deficient soil.
treatment: Apply fungicides containing Azoxystrobin or Propiconazole
Improve soil fertility, especially nitrogen and potassium
Brownspot.prevention: Maintain balanced soil nutrition and use resistant varieties.

Bacterialblight.description: Bacterial infection (Xanthomonas oryzae) causing yellowing and drying of leaves starting from the tips. Can cause significant yield loss.
Bacterialblight.prevention: Plant resistant varieties and avoid overhead irrigation during outbreaks.

Leafsmut.description: Fungal disease (Entyloma oryzae) that appears as small, slightly raised black spots (pustules) on both sides of the leaves.
Leafsmut.prevention: Use balanced fertilization and crop rotation.

Aloe_Anthracnose.description: Fungal infection causing sunken, dark brown to black spots on Aloe leaves, which may eventually lead to leaf rot.
treatment: Reduce humidity and avoid overhead watering
Aloe_Anthracnose.prevention: Ensure good air circulation and avoid overwatering.

Aloe_Healthy.description: The Aloe plant appears healthy with firm, green leaves.
Aloe_Healthy.prevention: Continue regular monitoring and avoid overwatering.

Aloe_LeafSpot.description: Small brown or black spots on leaves caused by fungal or bacterial pathogens.
treatment: Apply organic fungicides like neem oil
Aloe_LeafSpot.prevention: Avoid wetting the foliage during watering.

Aloe_Rust.description: Fungal disease characterized by raised orange or brown pustules on the underside of leaves.
Aloe_Rust.prevention: Keep foliage dry and provide adequate spacing between plants.

Aloe_Sunburn.description: Damage caused by intense direct sunlight, appearing as bleached, red, or brown patches on the leaves.
treatment: Move the plant to a shaded or filtered light area
Water the plant normally to help recovery
Do not remove leaves unless they are completely dried
Aloe_Sunburn.prevention: Gradually acclimate plants to higher light levels.

Cactus_Dactylopius_Opuntia.description: Infestation by cochineal insects (Dactylopius opuntiae), appearing as white, cottony tufts on the cactus pads.
treatment: Blast insects off with a strong stream of water
Apply insecticidal soap or neem oil
Use a brush dipped in alcohol to remove small clusters
Cactus_Dactylopius_Opuntia.prevention: Regularly inspect plants for white cottony spots.

Cactus_Healthy.description: The cactus appears healthy and free from pests or disease.
Cactus_Healthy.prevention: Provide adequate light and avoid overwatering.

Money_Plant_Bacterial_wilt_disease.description: Serious bacterial infection causing sudden wilting and yellowing of leaves, often leading to plant death.
treatment: Avoid using cuttings from infected plants
Money_Plant_Bacterial_wilt_disease.prevention: Use sterile potting mix and avoid waterlogged soil.

Money_Plant_Healthy.description: The Money Plant appears healthy with vibrant green, glossy leaves.
Money_Plant_Healthy.prevention: Provide bright indirect light and regular watering.

Money_Plant_Manganese_Toxicity.description: A physiological condition caused by excess manganese, leading to yellowing between veins and dark spots.
treatment: Flush the soil with clean, pH-balanced water
Adjust soil pH to a more alkaline range
Money_Plant_Manganese_Toxicity.prevention: Use balanced fertilizers and monitor soil pH.

Snake_Plant_Anthracnose.description: Fungal disease causing tan, water-soaked spots with dark borders on the leaves.
Snake_Plant_Anthracnose.prevention: Avoid overwatering and ensure good drainage.

Snake_Plant_Healthy.description: The Snake Plant appears healthy with upright, sturdy leaves.
Snake_Plant_Healthy.prevention: Allow soil to dry between waterings.

Snake_Plant_Leaf_Withering.description: Leaves becoming wrinkled or limp due to severe dehydration or root rot from overwatering.
treatment: Adjust watering schedule (water if dry, repot if rot)
Ensure the pot has drainage holes
Snake_Plant_Leaf_Withering.prevention: Maintain a consistent, appropriate watering schedule.

Spider_Plant_Fungal_leaf_spot.description: Small brown spots with yellow halos on leaves caused by fungal pathogens like Alternaria.
treatment: Apply neem oil or a copper fungicide
Increase spacing for better air circulation
Spider_Plant_Fungal_leaf_spot.prevention: Avoid overhead watering and keep humidity levels moderate.

Spider_Plant_Healthy.description: The Spider Plant appears healthy with long, arching green or variegated leaves.
Spider_Plant_Healthy.prevention: Provide bright indirect light and avoid fluoride-rich water.

Spider_Plant_Leaf_Tip_Necrosis.description: Browning or drying of leaf tips often caused by low humidity, salt buildup, or fluoride in tap water.
treatment: Use distilled or rainwater for watering
Flush soil to remove salt buildup
Spider_Plant_Leaf_Tip_Necrosis.prevention: Avoid using tap water with high fluoride content.

Unknown.description: The AI is not confident enough to identify a specific disease. This usually happens if the image is blurry, contains a non-supported crop, or is not a leaf.
treatment: Retake the photo in better lighting
Ensure the leaf is clearly visible and centered
Verify that the crop is supported by the app
Unknown.prevention: Keep the camera lens clean and avoid shadows on the leaf.
